text:
There once was a tiny ant who loved collecting seeds. One sunny morning, she discovered three magical seeds.

As days passed, she sewed them into cloth balls. When it grew big enough, she released them.

The seeds grew into trees. The biggest tree bore a berry red like her coat. One afternoon, she plucked them.

She was flying back home when a bird caught the berry into its bill. An owl saw them.

"Hush," it whispered. "Don't flytoo fast. We just want a snack."

They floated back home.

That was how birds spread apples. And from then on, little ants everywhere loved collecting birdberries.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
